rulings.Rulings: HEARING REQUIRED. PARTIES TO ADDRESS WHETHER COURT RETAINS JURISDICTION TO ENTERTAIN THIS MOTION. GENERALLY, AN APPEAL DIVESTS THE COURT OF JURISDICTION UNLESS THE REQUESTED RELIEF PERTAINS TO A COLLATERAL MATTER. WHILE A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NOT A RULING ON THE MERITS, see Cohen v. Board of Supervisors, (1985) 40 Cal.3d 277, 286, A JUDGMENT/PERMANENT INJUNCTION IS. See Chico Feminist Women's Health Center v. Scully, (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 230, 253. SINCE THERE IS CURRENTLY AN APPEAL OF THE ATTORNEY'S FEES MOTION AND WHO, IF ANYONE, IS THE PREVAILING PARTY, THE COURT CANNOT ALTER THE PERMANENT INJUNCTION. ANY ALTERATION WOULD AFFECT THE PREVAILING PARTY CALCULUS. (PB)
